seo.title: Mastra Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for Mastra agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using Mastra to build AI agents for citizen services, policy analysis, and national security workflows face stringent compliance demands under FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53. Without robust guardrails, common Mastra failure modes like unvalidated workflow outputs and stale memory retrieval can lead to national security breaches or civil liberties violations. Vex provides middleware compliance layers to ensure explainable, auditable, and secure agent operations.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring, audit logging, and secure data handling for cloud-based AI systems processing federal data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex middleware captures all Mastra workflow transitions and tool calls with tamper-proof audit trails compliant with FedRAMP logging standards.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk assessments, access controls, and validation of AI outputs to prevent unauthorized data exposure.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates workflow step outputs and blocks malformed agent responses before they propagate through Mastra pipelines.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and explainability for AI systems affecting citizens.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex generates compliance reports with decision traces and blocks non-explainable agent outputs in Mastra workflows.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI-generated content is accessible to individuals with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans Mastra agent outputs for WCAG 2.1 compliance before delivery to citizens.

industryRisks.name: Classified Data Exposure
industryRisks.description: Mastra agents accidentally processing classified documents through memory retrieval or tool calls.
industryRisks.example: Agent retrieves stale classified context from vector store and includes it in unclassified citizen response.

industryRisks.name: Non-Explainable Decisions
industryRisks.description: Workflow outputs lack audit trails making AI-assisted decisions non-appealable.
industryRisks.example: Benefits determination agent denies claim without decision reasoning trace.

industryRisks.name: Malformed Tool Outputs
industryRisks.description: Third-party integrations return invalid data causing cascading workflow failures.
industryRisks.example: FEMA integration returns null disaster data causing incorrect aid allocation.

industryRisks.name: Stale Context Injection
industryRisks.description: Outdated memory retrieval leads to decisions based on obsolete policy information.
industryRisks.example: Agent applies expired eligibility rules for federal assistance programs.

implementation.title: Add Vex Middleware to Mastra Pipeline
implementation.description: Install Vex SDK and wrap Mastra agent execution with compliance guardrails for workflow validation.

implementation.title: Validate Agent Tool Calls
implementation.description: Intercept Mastra tool calls to prevent classified data exposure and malformed responses.

implementation.title: Memory Retrieval Compliance Check
implementation.description: Prevent stale context injection by validating Mastra knowledge retrieval against current policies.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Mastra in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
The Department of Veterans Affairs deploys a Mastra-powered agent to process disability claims, integrating with legacy benefits systems and policy knowledge bases. The workflow retrieves veteran records, assesses eligibility using current regulations, calls external healthcare APIs, and generates appealable decisions. Without compliance guardrails, the agent experiences common Mastra failures: tool calls return malformed FHIR medical data, memory retrieval injects 18-month-old policy context, and workflow transitions pass unvalidated outputs to citizen-facing interfaces.

During peak processing, the agent incorrectly denies claims using expired rules and exposes PII through malformed API responses, triggering FISMA violations and public complaints. Congressional oversight demands audit trails that don't exist, halting deployment.
caseStudyScenario.outcome:
Vex middleware integration transforms the deployment. Workflow validation blocks 97% of malformed tool outputs before citizen impact. Memory retrieval guardrails reject stale policy context, ensuring current regulation application. All decisions generate NIST-compliant explainability reports with appeal links.

FedRAMP auditors verify complete audit trails across 250K+ transactions. The VA achieves FISMA High authorization, processes claims 40% faster, and eliminates compliance violations, restoring public trust.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Mastra agents today for instant FedRAMP and NIST 800-53 compliance.
